perturbation.original_text: 1.1 “ Confidential Information ” means any data, documents, specifications, and other information or materials in whatever form disc losed by the OSCE to the Recipient, whether documentary, orally, visually, or otherwise (includi ng computerised form) except information which was: (a) in the public domain at the time of disclos ure; (b) known to the Recipient prior to obtaining the same pursuant to this Confid entiality Agreement; or, (c) obtained by the Recipient from a third party who did not receive the same directly or indirectly from the OSCE under a confidentiality agreement with the OSCE, or (d) required by applicable law;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 “ Confidential Information ” means any data, documents, specifications, and other information or materials in whatever form disc losed by the OSCE to the Recipient, whether documentary, orally, visually, or otherwise (includi ng computerised form);
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the exceptions to the definition of 'Confidential Information,' all information disclosed by the OSCE is now considered confidential, regardless of its prior status (public domain, prior knowledge, etc.). This introduces a contradiction if the Recipient already possesses the information, but is now bound by the confidentiality agreement. Because now everything disclosed is considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In respect to the Confidential Inform ation, the Recipient hereby undertakes; 2.1.1 to hold in trust and confidence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without the express, prior written consent of the OSCE. 2.1.2 not to use the Confidential Information fo r any purpose whatsoever other than for the Permitted Purpose. 2.1.3 only to disclose the Conf idential Information to employees of companies directly or indirectly owned or controlled by the Recipi ent, consultants and/or advisors to whom disclosure is necessary for the Permitted Pur pose and then only on the understanding that such employees, consultants and/or advisors ar e expressly subject to the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. 2.1.4 not at any time other than to the extent that is necessary for the Permitted Purpose to make copies of or reduce the Confidential Informa tion to any electronic form or to store it in a database or other electronic media. 2.1.5 to comply with all requirements of th e OSCE for the security of the Confidential Information. 2.1.6 to treat Confidential Information as being confidential and proprietary to the OSCE by using the same degree of care, but in any case no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent unauthorised use, dissemination or publication thereof, as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. 2.1.7 upon demand of the OSCE, to promptly return all Confidential Information (in whatever medium), together with all copies th ereof or, where expressly authorised in writing by the OSCE, to destroy or delete all such C onfidential Information and shall confirm to the OSCE in writing that such destruction or deletion has taken place.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' with exceptions for information already in the public domain, previously known to the recipient, or obtained from a third party without a confidentiality agreement. The changed text removes these exceptions, making all information disclosed by the OSCE confidential. This contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the recipient's obligations regarding 'Confidential Information,' as it now applies to information that was previously excluded, therefore creating a clear and direct conflict regarding the scope and obligations related to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 “ Permitted Purpose ” means the use of the Confidential In formation to facilitate the Recipient performing the Services;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 “ Permitted Purpose ” means the use of the Confidential In formation for internal review;
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Permitted Purpose' from 'facilitating the Recipient performing the Services' to 'internal review,' the Recipient is now restricted in how they can use the Confidential Information. If 'Services' is still defined elsewhere in relation to a specific project, the change creates a contradiction because Recipient can no longer facilitate performing that service as the 'Permitted Purpose' only relates to the internal review.
perturbation.contradicted_text: not to use the Confidential Information fo r any purpose whatsoever other than for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Permitted Purpose' as 'facilitating the Recipient performing the Services'. The changed text narrows this definition to 'internal review.' The contradicted text states that the Confidential Information should not be used for any purpose other than the 'Permitted Purpose.' Therefore, the change creates a contradiction because the Recipient is now restricted to using the Confidential Information only for internal review, preventing them from using it to facilitate performing the Services, as initially intended and potentially defined elsewhere in the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 Non-Disclosure. The Parties agree that each of them shall not, without the prior consent of the other in writing, disclose to any third party th e terms of this Confidentiality Agreement, or the content, nature or extent of the discu ssions proceeding in connection herewith.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 Non-Disclosure. The Parties agree that each of them shall not disclose to any third party th e terms of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'without the prior consent of the other in writing, or the content, nature or extent of the discussions proceeding in connection herewith' from the non-disclosure clause, now creates a contradiction since it would be impossible to have discussions proceeding in connection with the contract to any extent without disclosing information to a 'third party'. Making the existence of any discussion on the contract with anyone a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 Non-Disclosure. The Parties agree that each of them shall not disclose to any third party th e terms of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in 9.1 allowed disclosure of the terms of the Confidentiality Agreement with prior written consent or regarding the content, nature, or extent of discussions. The changed text removes the allowance for discussion. This creates a contradiction because the explanation states that it would be impossible to have discussions proceeding in connection with the contract to any extent without disclosing information to a third party, therefore making any discussion a breach. This directly contradicts the intended purpose of discussing the contract, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text in 9.1.
